content: Epistemological boundary response: User's "规律?" challenge answered — explicit rejection of law-status with three missing conditions identified (out-of-sample testing, control group/specificity, complete mechanism), reclassification as "临床征象" (clinical signs/symptom clusters), and 30-day prospective scoring protocol proposed as upgrade path

Learned: The 7 features are now classified as "临床征象" (clinical signs) — empirically observed symptom clusters analogous to "fever+cough+chest pain suggests pneumonia" before large-sample validation became diagnostic criteria. The upgrade path requires: lock feature definitions → operationalize "turning point
Completed: Epistemological status of the 7-feature framework has been formally resolved: rejected as laws, accepted as clinical signs/hypotheses worth prospective testing. A 30-day scoring protocol with explicit threshold for upgrade (sensitivity > 0.7 AND specificity > 0.7 over ≥50 samples) has been proposed.
